item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If they increased the size of the building so greatly and are using it to contain SCP's, what happened to the rest of the town? Presumably there would still be people living there, so they'd have to be evacuated and amnestisized. Then there would still roads leading to the town which they'd have to destroy and excuse. Not to mention others who previously lived in the school or town that they'd have to track down and "re-inform".

Seems like a lot of work for another containment facility with only a few benefits and a huge risk of throwing a tantrum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's the cutest SCP I've read lately. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god…

It took a couple of reads to really get the full impact. Reading it, going through the discussion, reading some more. Not to understand it, but to really get the feel of it for myself.

Foundation contains an anomaly there. It notices (or whatever the headcanon is, I prefer that it was there and that it eventually came sense what was happening there), Foundation leaves. It finds a Magic 8-Ball, and because it's magic, it's anomalous and he wants to contain/protect it. So he keeps it safe.

And people come in, endangering the containment. And he wants to be like the people that were brave and responsible and so he eliminates the threat to containment.

And then McKnight gets there, and he realizes "oh no, I'm anomalous," so he begs them not to hurt him. And then they ask why he hurt those people…and he realizes that he might have done something very, very bad, and all he wanted was to be like the people he looked up to and

"i jus tryng to help you guys"

oh god my heart I love him so much

I find this really hard to put into a personal headcanon not because I can't create realities for it, but because I want it to be okay. It's going to realize at some point that they're not testing it. It's going to realize what they're doing. I don't want that to happen. So I avoid headcanoning it. Because my feels are so much feels (yes it's terrible grammar but it best represents my emotional state).

Really awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry, what are you talking about?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it'd be so funny if it reached the toaster and then it was like "wait… I suddenly feel like making bread"

:D

No, but seriously, thank you for this. I could never imagine something that I had a hand in making could become so popular.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That’s what made me like this too. He’s just doing his bestest to help people and its wholesome and cute and I’m just nervous what McKnight hasn’t planned for this cutie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: either statement is fine except in the most formal documents. The second is more correct, but harder to understand, especially once translated. The first is fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe my heart is cold and dead, but I didn't really get a lot out of this one. I'll come back later and see if I like it more.

Also possible grammar thing? "Under no circumstances is SCP-3970 to be told any items contained within Site-211 are anomalous in nature." Shouldn't it be 'told that any'?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps in his death his name was forgotten?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of emotion creating personality a lot, especially in the sense of a school. So many people learn and grow from a school that if a school developed a supernatural personality it would also grow and learn very fast like its past inhabitants. However, I am also a sucker for spirits possessing things and bettering themselves from other individuals traits and actions so i'm conflicted on which to believe here. Either way it's very obvious that you have made something really great here and it almost has limitless potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is the longest reply to a message i've ever seen

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this.

It's short-ish, sweet, straight to the point, and one of the best ways to do a Thaumiel article but without everyone acting in such a way that it has to be taken seriously and having the utmost importance and significance

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would die for SCP-3970.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
HNNNNNNNG.

In all seriousness, this is endearing and wonderful, but I also don't know what McKnight is up to and I don't think I like what he's doing. The tiny bit of mystery sticking there definitely makes me enjoy it just that much more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3970: it was magic so i had to keep it safe

Enthusiastic +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this was written to be open for later stories. There's still a lot that hasn't been explained… yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They know well what will happen if it finds out. That's why the containment procedures are mainly to prevent that. Luckily, unlike a normal child, it doesn't know what's normal and what's not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let's see if I have understood this correctly. The foundation has given an entity with the mind of a curious, fast learning and slightly murderous child a bunch of anomalous items to contain, and their best defense is "gee, I hope he never figures out what this stuff really is and that we have lied to him!" Ever seen a 10 year old that has figured out he has been deceived by his parents,? It is not a pretty sight.
Except from that I really liked this SCP. The characters where believable and I love the fact that the school tried to contain an eighth ball. It has a lot of opportunities for tales too. It is a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this! I thought the same as LordBlaze when I first read it, but then it said it didn't have a name… Just the right amount of creepy. Seems a little odd that the foundation would put so much trust in the entity, however, as the school has effectively been given free rein over the containment of a ton of anomalies. Could be potential for some later stories?

Also, quick nitpick- Glacier Peak is in the Cascade range, not the Rockies :)

EDIT- on a closer reread, free rein might not be the best term, as it doesn't know what is being contained within it- still, it holds the power in the situation no matter what it thinks. Also, the condition of the skip being out of the loop on the anomalous properties might change, as it is always learning… anyway. +1

item.title: MOD POST - INFO - OPEN
item.content:encoded:
To follow up on what my esteemed colleague said:

I'm too lazy to give constructive criticism

This is incredibly rude. If you don't have time to give feedback, don't. Being 'too lazy to give constructive feedback' literally violates the crit policy.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I was not being sarcastic. I actually thought that for a while while I was reading. It was not meant to be snarky, rude, or mean-spirited. I'll delete it if you want me to.

@Toa_Vine: I reached the conclusion when I had the following information:
1. The skip is a Foundation Site
2. The skip is sapient
3. The skip communicates through text of some sort
4. The skip is learning

Later information made this hypothesis improbable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Top floor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just our own headcanon. Who knows? There may be another explanation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers? Maybe more like headcanon?
Yea, we never actually agreed on an origin

I am relatively sure Endren is in the same camp with you on that one. (perhaps Endren himself can enlighten you more) Personally, I think it came from the "spirit" of the building. Like how the children of the building imbue the building with emotional energy. It is the same reason it can learn so fast, the idea of learning is also imbued in the building. I know this sounds mumbo-jumbo-y, but that is how I think it happened.

Actual Spoilers
I think we agreed on this front

Either way, The previous SCiP in the building awoke 3970 to it's new environment. It's first memories are the Foundation, and how they operate. So it observed and then praticed. Over time, intruders entered, and 3970 dealt with them the only way it knew how. Containing the threat.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: NanayaMode, not only is this crit snarky and rude, it's completely unhelpful as well. If you're "too lazy" to leave constructive criticism, being mean-spirited and sarcastic is not a good stand-in for actual critique that assists the authors. Don't pull this again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How did you come to that conclusion?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Erasing because I have poor communication skills and unintentionally said things that people find rude. I apologize to anyone I offended.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this. It's cute and wholesome. Also, you are welcome. :)

Also, I like how the title I had originally for the idea fits this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lots of anomalous schools on this site. Also lots of anomalies that make you go

(SCP-3970 creates a drawing of a smiling stick-figure in a lab-coat labeled "Dr. Underwood" standing inside a large rectangle labelled "Me".)

My heart.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Always nice to see more co-authored articles on the site! You two did a great job with the revisions. While I typically don't like casual notes in articles, I think the ending to this piece was a well-deserved nice touch, since there are definitely times when things work out well for the Foundation, and it makes sense that they'd want to celebrate those good times.

+1 from me here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess it has a lot of free time. Your version is quite creepy though, since it implies the child decided that 7 people were violating the site and just terminated them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My headcanon is that 3970 was a child attending the school that was killed and his spirit possessed the school and communicates through the chalkboards. How it learns so quickly? Spirit magic or somethin i guess xP

Great wholesome read and good luck in the future.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so quirky and fun, I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yay, it's the friendly building.

Oh no it's the utilitarian Foundation. D:

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The school's layout reminds my of my old school. Not sure how I feel about that.

The final step of Project 3970-θ is to commence immediately.

What did they do to this sweet little building?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Co-Authored with Endren. He wrote most of it while it was my idea, and I was always the first critique.

Thanks to these reviewers:
Zyn
fieldstone
Toa_Vine

And special thanks to Baronjoe for inspiring me after I totally misunderstood his idea thread.
